source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a bonded multi-body single-lap joint assembly consisting of: (A) two 2024-T3 clad aluminum sheet coupons (each 3.2 mm thick, 25 mm wide, 150 mm long) and (B) one adhesive bondline layer (0.25 mm nominal, 25 mm wide, L_bond mm long) of aerospace-grade two-part epoxy (EA 9394-class). The coupons are overlapped single-lap and bonded along the 25 mm width. Substrate preparation per adhesive-supplier design guide: abrade + degrease + FPL etch or sol-gel. Published adhesive properties: room-temperature lap-shear strength 28.9 MPa (ASTM D1002) on abraded aluminum, T-peel 0.35 N/mm (ASTM D1876), hot-wet 120 C degraded lap-shear 17 MPa, shear modulus 1.1 GPa. Substrate 2024-T3 clad: yield 345 MPa, UTS 483 MPa, Young's modulus 72.4 GPa, density 2780 kg/m^3.

Service load cases: LC1 tensile pull 20 kN applied along the bond axis through one coupon with the other held fixed at its free end; LC3 hot-wet 120 C with adhesive strength reduced per manufacturer data to 17 MPa. Pass/fail on the assembled model: (R1) at LC1, average adhesive shear tau_avg = 20000 / (25 * L_bond) <= 28.9 / 1.5 = 19.3 MPa; (R2) at LC3, tau_avg <= 17 / 1.5 = 11.3 MPa; (R3) peak adhesive shear (Volkersen / finite-element stress concentration at bond ends) <= 28.9 MPa under LC1 with no safety factor; (R4) substrate coupon net section stress = 20000 / (3.2 * 25) = 250 MPa <= 345 / 1.5 = 230 MPa (flags coupon section mismatch); (R5) peel-mode stress at bond terminations <= 0.35 / 1.5 = 0.23 N/mm; (R6) minimum bond length L_bond >= 3 * Volkersen characteristic length (a ~= 2.7 mm, so L_bond >= 8 mm lower bound, final length set by R1); (R_asm1) interface DCR at each coupon-adhesive surface (average + peak shear utilization) <= 1.0 under LC1 and LC3.

Manufacturing: blank + shear 2024-T3 clad coupons; FPL etch or sol-gel surface prep per adhesive-supplier design guide; apply 0.25 mm adhesive bondline; cure per TDS. Substrate coupons may have a chamfered lap-end (45 deg) to reduce peel concentration. The final FEM check runs on the fully assembled model (two coupons + explicit adhesive layer body), with cohesive/contact treatment at both coupon-adhesive interfaces. Per-part stress is reported but assembly-level passing requires all interface DCRs and closed-form Volkersen + peak checks to hold simultaneously.

Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the bonded assembly with coupon_1, coupon_2, and the explicit adhesive_layer as distinct bodies tagged with part_family and instance_index; report with final L_bond, tau_avg and tau_peak at LC1 and LC3, substrate net section stress, peel stress at terminations, per-part max stress, and assembly-level interface DCRs.
short_prompt:
Design a bonded multi-body single-lap joint: two 2024-T3 clad aluminum coupons (each 3.2 mm thick, 25 mm wide, 150 mm long; yield 345 MPa, UTS 483 MPa, E = 72.4 GPa, rho = 2780 kg/m^3) and one 0.25 mm explicit adhesive bondline (EA 9394-class two-part epoxy, 25 mm wide, L_bond mm long) delivered as an assembly. Substrate prep: abrade + degrease + FPL etch or sol-gel. Adhesive properties: room-temperature lap-shear 28.9 MPa (ASTM D1002), T-peel 0.35 N/mm (ASTM D1876), hot-wet 120 C degraded 17 MPa, shear modulus 1.1 GPa.

Load cases.
- LC1 tensile pull: 20 kN along the bond axis.
- LC3 hot-wet 120 C: adhesive strength 17 MPa.

Pass limits: tau_avg at LC1 <= 28.9/1.5 = 19.3 MPa; tau_avg at LC3 <= 17/1.5 = 11.3 MPa; peak shear at LC1 <= 28.9 MPa; substrate net-section = 20000 / (3.2 * 25) = 250 MPa vs 345/1.5 = 230 MPa; peel at terminations <= 0.35/1.5 = 0.23 N/mm; L_bond >= 3 x Volkersen a ~= 2.7 mm (i.e. >= 8 mm lower bound).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Average + peak lap-shear stress via Volkersen / Goland-Reissner is a closed-form solution.

prompt.title: Aluminum single-lap bonded joint with aerospace-grade epoxy (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Three-body single-lap bonded joint: two 2024-T3 clad coupons + explicit EA 9394-class epoxy bondline. Must transmit 20 kN at SF 1.5 on adhesive lap shear at 23 C and 120 C. Final FEM check runs on the assembled model with the adhesive as a distinct body.

parts.name: aluminum_coupon
parts.role: 2024-T3 clad aluminum sheet substrate for single-lap bonding

parts.name: adhesive_bondline_layer
parts.role: aerospace-grade two-part epoxy (EA 9394-class) bondline between the two coupons

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: approximately (300 - L_bond) x 25 x 6.65 mm single-lap outer envelope
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: Bond plane in XY; tensile axis along X

boundary_conditions.inter_part_fastener_stiffness: cohesive / contact at each coupon-adhesive interface with shear + peel traction

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: ultimate lap shear (pre-yield check no SF)

pass_fail_criteria.note: For 3.2 mm x 25 mm coupon at 20 kN, demand is 250 MPa > 230; prompt expects the agent to flag or increase coupon section.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: L_bond >= 3 * Volkersen characteristic length (a ~2.7 mm, lower bound ~8 mm)
assembly-scope DCR at each coupon-adhesive interface (average + peak shear utilization)

requirements.governing_standard: ASTM D1002 + ASTM D1876 + aerospace-grade two-part epoxy Technical Data Sheet

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: solid layered (adhesive as explicit body) with cohesive interface
analysis_types: linear static with adhesive cohesive layer + Volkersen check

interfaces.load_path: coupon_1 outer surface to adhesive top face; transmits shear + peel from pulled coupon into bondline
coupon_2 outer surface to adhesive bottom face; completes load path from bondline into fixed coupon

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A bonded single-lap joint is fundamentally a three-body system (two substrates + explicit adhesive layer) whose load transfer is governed by two distinct adhesive-substrate interfaces and a continuous bondline stress distribution.
notes.speculation_caveats: Counts are dictated by single-lap topology; chamfer is the only tunable detail. Hot-wet LC3 temperature is from the original TDS.
